title: Bridge Essay: The Enactive Approach to Habits: New Concepts for the Cognitive Science of Bad Habits and Addiction.
concept: Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.
**Phase context:** Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

**The Enactive Approach to Habits: New Concepts for the Cognitive Science of Bad Habits and Addiction.** — Front Psychol (2019). DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2019.00301. PMID: 30863334. Habits are the topic of a venerable history of research that extends back to antiquity, yet they were originally disregarded by the cognitive sciences. They started to become the focus of interdisciplinary research in the 1990s, but since then there has been a stalemate between those who approach habits as a kind of bodily automatism or as a kind of mindful action. This implicit mind-body dualism is ready to be overcome with the rise of interest in embodied, embedded, extended, and enactive (4E)

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 3 (Mind as Computer Critique). Evaluation: Good (score=2.6)
